title: Replace print-based ASCII rendering with Rich library for terminal UI
source: Python Rich library best practices (github.com/Textualize/rich) — widely adopted standard for modern terminal UIs
description: visual.py builds all UI with raw print() and manual string padding/ljust(). This leads to broken alignment when emoji widths vary across terminals, no color support, and fragile box-drawing. The Rich library provides Tables, Panels, Progress bars, Live displays, and Tree views with automatic width handling and true-color support — all zero-config.
proposed_changes: Add `rich` to dependencies. Refactor visual.py to use: (1) `rich.table.Table` for team boards and sprint dashboards instead of manual box-drawing with ljust padding, (2) `rich.progress.Progress` for progress bars instead of manual █/░ construction, (3) `rich.panel.Panel` for agent status boxes with auto-width, (4) `rich.live.Live` context manager for animated portal frames instead of print+sleep+clear-screen, (5) `rich.tree.Tree` for mixed-mode team hierarchy visualization. This fixes the emoji-width alignment bugs (lines 233, 274 where ljust doesn't account for double-width emoji) and adds ANSI color theming (green portal, yellow status, red errors).
